blockingIssues.description: Module-level `let` bindings are still order-dependent during typechecking because resolver collects top-level lets as globals, but typecheck only makes an `ItemLet` visible after it reaches that item in source order. Functions declared before a global shared-state binding can therefore resolve but then fail typechecking, leaving the advertised module-level CRUD state support incomplete.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: When splitting a milestone into multiple implementation features, keep each feature's description and expected behavior aligned with the code it actually lands rather than moving the user-visible behavior into an earlier commit and leaving a later feature as mostly test-only follow-up.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `http-crud-state-observability-and-error-paths` review found that commit `ae916b4` mostly extends tests while the runtime behavior described by the feature was already present in `ec0c4cb`, which made review and milestone accounting harder.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify whether step 8 requires running every command listed in `.factory/services.yaml` or only the relevant validator/build commands for a feature, and explicitly call out whether informational commands like `cli_version` and `go_version` are mandatory.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The `http-crud-state-observability-and-error-paths` review noted that `.factory/skills/compiler-worker/SKILL.md` says to run the mission-level commands from `.factory/services.yaml`, but the reviewed handoff omitted `cli_version` and `go_version` while still marking the procedure as followed.
